mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic of the mean return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days exclusively during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annually, or 20-day returns are negative in greater than 55% of high-INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days as markets price in higher corporate earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with decelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate monetary tightening outpacing labor market strength, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 signal healthy labor demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days only when unemployment remains below 5.0%.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 150k yields negative 20-day returns when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample Sharpe ratio by greater than 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt supply-side contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days due to earnings downgrade fears.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold condition shows no statistical edge over a simple moving average baseline.
